semantic.role: Composite task orchestrating the post-commit derivation pipeline: runs aggregation, reverse-indexing, knowledge-graph, and view-derivation sub-tasks, then writes the spine README and regenerates all view artifacts.
responsibilities: Executes the AggregationTask via TaskRunner to produce aggregated semantic data.
Executes the ReverseIndexingTask via TaskRunner to build reverse indexes.
Executes the KnowledgeGraphTask via TaskRunner to construct the module dependency graph.
Executes the ViewDerivationTask via TaskRunner to derive view artifacts.
Writes the `.spine/README.md` control plane document with instructions for AI agents.
Clears previous view artifacts using ViewService.clearViewArtifacts.
Regenerates all view artifacts by running the view regeneration function for each view definition (VIEW_DEFINITIONS).
Saves the final god document via ctx.outputManager.saveGodDoc.
outOfScope: Does not parse CLI commands or handle user input.
Does not implement the logic of individual derivation sub-tasks (delegates to dedicated task classes).
Does not manage runtime sessions, checkpoints, or scan policies.
Does not define view definitions or rendering logic.
invariants: The sub-tasks are always executed in the fixed order: aggregation, reverse-indexing, knowledge-graph, view-derivation.
After execution completes, the spine README and all view artifacts are guaranteed to reflect the current codebase state.
Each sub-task is executed through the TaskRunner, respecting pipeline execution semantics and error handling.
The task operates within the CommitStageOutput context and produces no return value (void).

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a single, well-defined entry point for all post-commit derivation work, ensuring data consistency across derived artifacts after each commit.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Refactored as part of infrastructure cleanup to remove legacy CLI aliases and rebuild documentation; the core orchestration logic remains intact.

publicSurface.description: Exported class that orchestrates the full post-commit derivation pipeline. External modules instantiate and run this task to regenerate all derived data (aggregation, indexes, knowledge graph, views) after a commit.
